title: Share of the population with anxiety disorders
subtitle: Estimated share of people with  [anxiety disorders](#dod:anxiety_disorders), whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.
caption:
Share of the population with anxiety disorders

Estimated share of people with  anxiety disorders, whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated share of the population with anxiety disorders in 2023. The legend ranges from 0% to 10%, with color bands marking intervals at 2%, 4%, 6%, and 8%. The darkest blue shade, representing values between 8% and 10%, is visible in Australia, New Zealand, Brazil, and several countries in Western Europe including Spain, Portugal, and the United Kingdom. Iran also appears in this highest visible band. A mid-range blue, corresponding roughly to the 4% to 6% interval, covers the United States, Canada, and much of South America outside of Brazil. In Africa, a cluster of nations in the central and western regions sits in these mid-range shades. Lighter shades of blue, indicating shares between 2% and 4%, are distributed across Russia, China, and large portions of Eastern Europe and Asia. The lightest visible band, representing 0% to 2%, appears in parts of Southeast Asia and scattered territories in Africa. Greenland and a few other small island nations are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data. The visual distribution shows the highest estimated shares concentrated in Oceania, South America, and Western Europe, while lower shares are observed across much of Asia and parts of Africa.
